macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Q1 GDP Growth Slows to 2% as Inflation Accelerates Above Fed Target
items.summary: First-quarter GDP came in at 2%, up from 0.5% in Q4 but below analyst forecasts. Meanwhile, PCE inflation jumped to 3.5% annually in March, the largest increase since May 2023, driven partly by surging gas prices amid the Iran war.

items.title: Federal Reserve Holds Interest Rates Steady; Market Expects Extended Pause
items.summary: The Fed left its benchmark interest rate unchanged in the 3.5%-3.75% range, with financial markets now expecting rates to remain steady well into 2026 as inflation concerns persist.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge 24% in March; Oil Hits 4-Year High on Iran War Concerns
items.summary: National average gas prices jumped 24% in March as global oil prices reached over $126 per barrel, with Brent crude doubling since US-Israeli attacks on Iran began and West Texas Intermediate crude up roughly 90% due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

items.title: Jobless Claims Hit Lowest Level Since 1969; Labor Market Remains Resilient
items.summary: Weekly jobless claims data released Thursday reached their lowest level since 1969, with unemployment remaining historically low and the labor market characterized as a 'low-hire, low-fire' environment.

items.title: Consumer Spending Expands at 1.6% Annual Pace; Tax Refunds and AI Boom Provide Support
items.summary: Consumer spending, which accounts for 70% of US economic activity, grew at a 1.6% annual rate in Q1, supported by tax refunds and increased business investment in artificial intelligence.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Hit $38.64M, Primarily Short Traders Wiped Out
items.summary: The crypto market saw $38.64 million in futures liquidations over the past 24 hours, with shorts dominating losses on Bitcoin ($16.32M), Ethereum ($17.80M), and Dogecoin ($4.52M). This shakeout targeted short sellers amid market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Climbs Above $76K Amid Big Tech Earnings Recovery
items.summary: Bitcoin rose more than 1% to $76,400 in the past 24 hours, signaling a mild bullish trend near $77,000, while Ethereum hovered around $2,200-$2,300 with neutral momentum. Trading volume dropped sharply as markets digested softer U.S. Q1 GDP data.

items.title: Gemini Secures Major CFTC License for Derivatives Trading
items.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Gemini received authorization from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) for derivatives trading, marking a significant regulatory milestone. This enhances Gemini's operations in the U.S. market.

items.title: Ripple Opens New Middle East & Africa HQ Amid Regional Tensions
items.summary: Ripple established a new headquarters for the Middle East and Africa despite ongoing US-Israel-Iran conflicts. This expansion underscores the company's commitment to regional growth.

items.title: Meta Offers USDC Payouts to Creators on Solana and Polygon
items.summary: Meta Platforms began paying select creators in Circle’s USDC stablecoin via Solana and Polygon networks. This move re-enters Meta into crypto, focusing on stablecoin integrations for payouts.

items.title: Tether Proposes Major Merger with 21 Capital, Strike, and Elektron Energy
items.summary: Tether announced a proposed three-way merger involving 21 Capital (XXI), Strike, and Elektron Energy, potentially reshaping Bitcoin treasury operations. Stablecoins continue to gain prominence in global finance.

items.title: SUI Unlocks 74M Tokens Worth $270M
items.summary: SUI network unlocked 74 million tokens valued at $270 million, with market impact being closely monitored by investors. This event could influence price dynamics in the coming hours.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Secures 10GW U.S. AI Compute Capacity Ahead of Schedule
items.summary: OpenAI's Stargate initiative reached its 10 GW target over a year early, with the first site in Abilene, Texas, and NVIDIA committing up to $100 billion for deployments starting in late 2026. Additional sites are planned in Texas, New Mexico, Wisconsin, and Michigan.

items.title: Nvidia Shares Surge on Strong AI Forecast
items.summary: Nvidia's positive earnings forecast calmed concerns over an AI bubble, boosting shares 5% in extended trading and adding $220 billion in market value after a prior 8% drop amid three-year gains of 1,200%.

items.title: Google Opens Major AI Hardware Center in Taiwan
items.summary: Google launched its largest AI infrastructure hardware engineering center outside the U.S. in Taiwan, signaling long-term investment commitment as stated by President Lai Ching-te.

items.title: Luma AI Raises $900M at $4B Valuation for Video Generation
items.summary: Video generation platform Luma AI secured $900 million in funding, valuing it at $4 billion, to scale its capital-intensive operations led by Indian-American CEO Amit Jain.

items.title: AMD, Cisco, and Saudi's Humain Launch AI Joint Venture with 100MW Data Center
items.summary: The joint venture starts with a 100-megawatt data center in Saudi Arabia, already contracted to supply generative video startup Luma AI.

items.title: Brookfield Launches $100B AI Infrastructure Program
items.summary: Brookfield announced a $100 billion AI infrastructure initiative anchored by a new fund targeting $10 billion in equity commitments.

items.title: Lumai Debuts Iris Optical System for Efficient LLM Inference
items.summary: Oxford spinout Lumai launched the Iris Nova optical inference server on April 28, claiming 90% lower energy use than GPUs for real-time billion-parameter LLMs via hybrid optical-digital design.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Unified AI Agent Model
items.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni integrates vision, audio, language, and video processing, enabling 9x faster and cheaper AI agents with better context retention.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup
items.summary: Microsoft has unveiled the full list of games coming to Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025, ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase.

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Announces New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture revealed their latest over-the-top action game, blending No More Heroes and Dead Rising vibes.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Reveals ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 has announced a new Force of Law Warbond, allowing players to access fresh content in the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Modder Releases Loader to Turn PS5 into Linux System
items.summary: A modder has released a loader enabling Linux on PS5, highlighting Linux's expanding presence in gaming hardware.

items.title: Subnautica 2 Enters Early Access on May 14
items.summary: The highly anticipated Subnautica sequel will launch in early access on May 14 after significant development drama.

items.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Releases October 6 on PC, PS5, Xbox
items.summary: Star Wars: Galactic Racer is set for release on October 6 across PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, with a $160 collector's edition featuring a landspeeder model.

items.title: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 Adds PS VR2 Support
items.summary: Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 now supports PlayStation VR2, with performance improvements in the latest update.

items.title: Xbox Adds Selective Quick Resume Feature
items.summary: Xbox users can now choose which games use Quick Resume as part of new console and PC app updates.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced and Playable Immediately
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announced Nioh 3 during Sony’s State of Play, allowing soulslike fans to play it right away.

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel 'The Scarlet Engagement' Revealed
items.summary: Developer ArtPlay unveiled The Scarlet Engagement, a new Bloodstained entry that diverges significantly from the original, at June’s PlayStation State of Play.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Reports Q2 Revenue Up 17% to $111.18B, Authorizes $100B Share Buyback
items.summary: Apple's March quarter saw record revenue, iPhone sales, and EPS, with net income up 19% to $29.6B despite chip supply constraints limiting iPhone demand. Services revenue hit a new all-time high.

items.title: Intel Shares Surge 114% in April, Market Cap Tops $470B in Record Month
items.summary: Intel's stock jumped to a record high on April 24, marking the chipmaker's best month ever and pushing its market capitalization past $470 billion.

items.title: Google Rolls Out Gemini to Cars with Google Built-In, Replacing Google Assistant
items.summary: Google is updating vehicles with Google built-in to replace Google Assistant with the more conversational Gemini, starting with English in the US.

items.title: Researchers Patch Linux CopyFail Vulnerability Allowing Unprivileged Admin Access
items.summary: A now-patched Linux flaw called CopyFail enabled unprivileged users to gain admin access, though many distributions are yet to apply fixes.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has built and deployed the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.
